entities.name: SLC7A11
synonyms: cystine transporter solute carrier family 7 member 11

entities.name: SLC3A2
synonyms: cystine transporter solute carrier family 3 member 2

3Department of Otolaryngology, New York Medical College, Valhalla, NY 10595, USA
4Department of Dermatology, New York Medical College, Valhalla, NY 10595, USA
Ferroptosis—The "Double-Edged Sword" in Cancer: Mechanisms of Tumor Suppression/Resistance and Therapeutic Manipulation

evidence_entities.text: Ferroptosis is an iron-dependent form of programmed cell death that is driven by the accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS), leading to the formation of membrane-damaging lipid peroxides
The execution of ferroptosis is classified as the oxidation of phospholipids containing polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs)

Iron in its ferrous state (Fe2+) is required for the Fenton reaction, a chemical process in which ferrous iron ions are used in the decomposition of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) to create reactive hydroxyl radicals
GSH is oxidized via glutathione peroxidase 4 (GPX4) to form oxidized GSSG in order to neutralize harmful lipid peroxides, and thus protect the cell from membrane damage and cell death
p53 downregulates SLC7A11 expression, a key component of the xCT system
Erastin acts through the inhibition of the xCT antiporter (or Xc− system) composed of both SLC7A11 and SLC3A2
RSL3 is another induction molecule that directly inhibits GPX4 by bypassing the need for GSH depletion
IFN-γ, which is secreted by CD8+ T cells, can downregulate the expression of the Xc− system (which includes SLC7A11) in tumor cells, making them more susceptible to ferroptosis

notes: This paper provides a comprehensive review of ferroptosis mechanisms in cancer, highlighting its dual role as both tumor suppressor and promoter
The review emphasizes the therapeutic potential of targeting ferroptosis pathways in cancer treatment
Multiple biomarkers and therapeutic targets are discussed, including GPX4, SLC7A11, and various small molecule inhibitors
